basset_hound_ownership.other_breeds_owned_line_1: Currently own one mixed-breed rescue (Beagle/Labrador mix) and previously owned a Golden Retriever and a Beagle.
basset_hound_ownership.other_breeds_owned_line_2: Also fostered two senior Dachshunds through a local rescue over the past three years.

breeding_experience_and_practices.how_often_have_you_bred_a_litter: N/A – I have never bred Basset Hounds or any other breed; all of my dogs have been pets or rescues, and I have no plans to breed.

club_memberships_and_judging.kennel_clubs_belong_to_line_1: Member, Rocky Mountain Hound Fanciers Association (since 2021); volunteer coordinator for educational outreach events and beginner handling workshops.
club_memberships_and_judging.kennel_clubs_belong_to_line_2: Also a general member of the Colorado Canine Sports & Training Club, where I regularly participate in obedience and rally classes with my Basset Hounds.

bhca_application_motivation.reasons_for_applying_bhca_line_1: I am applying for BHCA membership because over the past eight years my Basset Hounds have become the center of my dog-related activities, and I want to connect with experienced breeders, exhibitors, and mentors who are committed to preserving the health, temperament, and classic character of the breed. I currently share my home with two Bassets—"Mabel," a 6-year-old spayed female from a small, ethical breeder in Nebraska, and "Gus," a 3-year-old neutered male who came to us through Colorado Basset Hound Rescue—and I want to deepen my understanding of responsible ownership, structure and movement, and best practices for long-term care (particularly for backs, joints, and weight management).
bhca_application_motivation.reasons_for_applying_bhca_line_2: In addition to learning, I would like to give back to the Basset community by helping with rescue transports, volunteering at events, and supporting public education about the breed’s real needs and limitations so fewer Bassets end up in rescue due to mismatched expectations. I am especially interested in conformation and companion performance sports (rally, scent work, and trick dog titles) and would value access to BHCA-approved mentors and resources to help me handle my own dogs more confidently, evaluate pedigrees and health testing, and advocate for ethical breeding practices even though I do not plan to breed myself. Being part of BHCA feels like the most direct way to contribute to the future of the breed I love while surrounding myself with people who share the same long-term, thoughtful commitment to Basset Hounds.

additional_comments.comment_box_additional_info_line_1: I live in Fort Collins, Colorado, with my spouse and our two Basset Hounds (Mabel and Gus) plus one mixed-breed rescue dog, and our day-to-day life is very dog-centered. I work remotely as a senior project manager for an environmental consulting firm, which allows me to be home with the dogs most of the day. This has made it easier for us to prioritize training, daily sniff-walks, and structured enrichment (puzzle feeders, nose work games in the backyard, and short training sessions spread throughout the day). We have a fully fenced 1/3-acre yard with 5-foot wooden fencing, and we are extremely careful about gate security because both of our Bassets are typical scent-driven escape artists if given a chance. All of our dogs are kept as indoor family companions who sleep in our bedroom (Gus prefers his crate; Mabel rotates between a dog bed and the foot of our bed).
additional_comments.comment_box_additional_info_line_2: Medically, we are very proactive: both Bassets are on a strict weight-management plan supervised by our veterinarian, are up to date on vaccinations and heartworm prevention, and see a veterinary chiropractor as needed for mild lower-back stiffness after very active weekends. I am comfortable with the realities of long-term care for a dwarf, long-backed breed and budget accordingly for emergency care and potential orthopedic issues. Over the last three years I have volunteered regularly with Colorado Basset Hound Rescue, helping with home checks, post-adoption follow-up calls, and occasional short-term fostering (mostly seniors needing a quiet place to decompress). I am applying to BHCA with the mindset that this is a long-term commitment; my goal is to be an active, engaged member who participates in events when possible, continues to learn, and serves as a responsible, honest ambassador for Basset Hounds in my local community.
